tool_input.description: Check torchvision availability and cached weights
Dry-run pip install torchvision to check resolved versions
Dry-run pip install torchvision to check resolved versions

tool_input.command:
.venv/bin/python - <<'EOF'
import torch
sd = torch.load("model/pretrained/_full_mnv3s.pth", weights_only=True)
trunc = {k: v for k, v in sd.items()
         if k.startswith("features.") and int(k.split(".")[1]) <= 8}
torch.save(trunc, "model/pretrained/mnv3s_features8.pt")
n = sum(v.numel() for v in trunc.values())
print("tensors:", len(trunc), "params:", n)
import os; print("bytes:", os.path.getsize("model/pretrained/mnv3s_features8.pt"))
os.remove("model/pretrained/_full_mnv3s.pth")
EOF
tool_input.description: Truncate checkpoint to features 0-8 and save in model/pretrained

tool_input.command:
.venv/bin/python - <<'PYEOF'
import json, math

INK, MUT, FAINT, ACC, OCH = "#111111", "#6b6a60", "#9b998c", "#8c2f1f", "#8a6a1e"
FONT = "Palatino,Georgia,serif"
IC = 112
LOSS_Y = 292


def txt(x, y, s, size=10, color=MUT, w=400, anchor="middle", style=""):
    return (f"<text x='{x:.0f}' y='{y:.0f}' font-family='{FONT}' font-size='{size}' "
            f"fill='{color}' font-weight='{w}' text-anchor='{anchor}' {style}>{s}</text>")


def cap(xc, y, name, sub=None, color=MUT, name_color=None):
    out = [txt(xc, y, name, 10.5, name_color or (INK if color == MUT else color), 600)]
    if sub:
        out.append(txt(xc, y + 12, sub, 9.5, color))
    return "".join(out)


def harrow(x1, x2, y, label=None, color=FAINT):
    out = [f"<line x1='{x1}' y1='{y}' x2='{x2 - 5}' y2='{y}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='1'/>",
           f"<path d='M {x2},{y} l -6,-3 v 6 Z' fill='{color}'/>"]
    if label:
        out.append(txt((x1 + x2) / 2, y - 6, label, 9, FAINT, style="font-style='italic'"))
    return "".join(out)


def leader(x1, y1, x2, y2, color):
    return (f"<line x1='{x1}' y1='{y1}' x2='{x2}' y2='{y2}' stroke='{color}' "
            f"stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='2 4'/>")


def slab(x, s, d, color=INK):
    y = IC - s / 2
    dx, dy = d, d * 0.55
    out = [
        f"<polygon points='{x},{y} {x+dx},{y-dy} {x+dx+s},{y-dy} {x+s},{y}' "
        f"fill='#00000010' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='1'/>",
        f"<polygon points='{x+s},{y} {x+dx+s},{y-dy} {x+dx+s},{y-dy+s} {x+s},{y+s}' "
        f"fill='#0000001c' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='1'/>",
        f"<rect x='{x}' y='{y}' width='{s}' height='{s}' fill='#00000006' "
        f"stroke='{color}' stroke-width='1.2'/>",
    ]
    return "".join(out), s + dx


def imgsq(x, s, color=INK):
    y = IC - s / 2
    out = [f"<rect x='{x}' y='{y}' width='{s}' height='{s}' fill='#00000008' "
           f"stroke='{color}' stroke-width='1.4'/>"]
    n = 6
    for i in range(1, n):
        out.append(f"<line x1='{x + i * s / n:.0f}' y1='{y}' x2='{x + i * s / n:.0f}' "
                   f"y2='{y + s}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='0.4' opacity='0.3'/>")
        out.append(f"<line x1='{x}' y1='{y + i * s / n:.0f}' x2='{x + s}' "
                   f"y2='{y + i * s / n:.0f}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='0.4' opacity='0.3'/>")
    for (a, b, o) in ((1, 2, .35), (3, 1, .5), (2, 4, .4), (4, 3, .3), (0, 4, .25), (4, 0, .3)):
        out.append(f"<rect x='{x + a * s / n:.0f}' y='{y + b * s / n:.0f}' "
                   f"width='{s / n:.0f}' height='{s / n:.0f}' fill='{color}' opacity='{o * 0.35}'/>")
    return "".join(out), s


def bar(x, h, color=INK, ticks=9):
    y = IC - h / 2
    out = [f"<rect x='{x}' y='{y}' width='7' height='{h}' fill='none' "
           f"stroke='{color}' stroke-width='1.2'/>"]
    for i in range(1, ticks):
        out.append(f"<line x1='{x}' y1='{y + i * h / ticks:.1f}' x2='{x + 7}' "
                   f"y2='{y + i * h / ticks:.1f}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='0.5' opacity='0.5'/>")
    return "".join(out), 7


def fan(x1, h1, x2, h2, color=FAINT, n=5):
    out = []
    for i in range(n):
        ya = IC - h1 / 2 + (i + 0.5) * h1 / n
        for j in range(n):
            yb = IC - h2 / 2 + (j + 0.5) * h2 / n
            out.append(f"<line x1='{x1}' y1='{ya:.1f}' x2='{x2}' y2='{yb:.1f}' "
                       f"stroke='{color}' stroke-width='0.5' opacity='0.45'/>")
    return "".join(out)


def gridsq(x, s, n, color=INK, bumps=(), yc=None, lw=1.2):
    yc = IC if yc is None else yc
    y = yc - s / 2
    out = [f"<rect x='{x}' y='{y}' width='{s}' height='{s}' fill='none' "
           f"stroke='{color}' stroke-width='{lw}'/>"]
    for i in range(1, n):
        out.append(f"<line x1='{x + i * s / n:.1f}' y1='{y}' x2='{x + i * s / n:.1f}' "
                   f"y2='{y + s}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='0.45' opacity='0.5'/>")
        out.append(f"<line x1='{x}' y1='{y + i * s / n:.1f}' x2='{x + s}' "
                   f"y2='{y + i * s / n:.1f}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='0.45' opacity='0.5'/>")
    for (gx, gy, o) in bumps:
        out.append(f"<rect x='{x + gx * s / n:.1f}' y='{y + gy * s / n:.1f}' "
                   f"width='{s / n:.1f}' height='{s / n:.1f}' fill='{color}' fill-opacity='{o}'/>")
    return "".join(out), s


BUMP = ((5, 2, .55), (4, 2, .28), (5, 1, .28), (6, 2, .18), (5, 3, .18), (2, 5, .12), (1, 6, .1))


def crosspt(x, y, color, r=7):
    return (f"<line x1='{x - r}' y1='{y}' x2='{x + r}' y2='{y}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='1.4'/>"
            f"<line x1='{x}' y1='{y - r}' x2='{x}' y2='{y + r}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='1.4'/>"
            f"<circle cx='{x}' cy='{y}' r='{r * 0.55:.1f}' fill='none' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='1.4'/>"
            f"<circle cx='{x}' cy='{y}' r='1.5' fill='{color}'/>")


def converge(gx, gs, tx, color, n=8):
    out = []
    src = []
    y0 = IC - gs / 2
    for gyy in range(n):
        for gxx in range(n):
            if (gxx + gyy) % 2:
                continue
            src.append((gx + (gxx + 0.5) * gs / n, y0 + (gyy + 0.5) * gs / n))
    for (sx, sy) in src:
        out.append(f"<line x1='{sx:.0f}' y1='{sy:.0f}' x2='{tx}' y2='{IC}' "
                   f"stroke='{color}' stroke-width='0.55' opacity='0.4'/>")
    out.append(crosspt(tx, IC, color))
    return "".join(out)


def gauge(x, yc, color=MUT, r=14):
    a = math.radians(55)
    return (f"<path d='M {x - r},{yc} A {r} {r} 0 0 1 {x + r},{yc}' fill='none' "
            f"stroke='{color}' stroke-width='1.2'/>"
            f"<line x1='{x}' y1='{yc}' x2='{x + (r - 3) * math.cos(a):.1f}' "
            f"y2='{yc - (r - 3) * math.sin(a):.1f}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='1.4'/>"
            f"<circle cx='{x}' cy='{yc}' r='1.6' fill='{color}'/>")


def lanes():
    return (txt(8, 26, "INFERENCE PATH — WHAT FLIES", 9, FAINT, 600, "start",
                "letter-spacing='1.8'")
            + txt(8, LOSS_Y - 34, "TRAINING SIGNALS — NEVER FLY", 9, OCH, 600, "start",
                  "letter-spacing='1.8'"))


def kernel_proj(x1, s1, x2, s2, color):
    k = max(5, s1 * 0.30)
    kx, ky = x1 + s1 * 0.60, IC - s1 / 2 + s1 * 0.18
    c = max(3, s2 * 0.14)
    cx, cy = x2 + s2 * 0.22, IC - s2 / 2 + s2 * 0.26
    out = [f"<rect x='{kx:.1f}' y='{ky:.1f}' width='{k:.1f}' height='{k:.1f}' "
           f"fill='none' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='0.9'/>",
           f"<rect x='{cx:.1f}' y='{cy:.1f}' width='{c:.1f}' height='{c:.1f}' "
           f"fill='{color}' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='none'/>"]
    for (px, py) in ((kx, ky), (kx + k, ky), (kx, ky + k), (kx + k, ky + k)):
        out.append(f"<line x1='{px:.1f}' y1='{py:.1f}' x2='{cx + c / 2:.1f}' "
                   f"y2='{cy + c / 2:.1f}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='0.55' "
                   f"opacity='0.45'/>")
    return "".join(out)


def frame_part():
    g, w = imgsq(26, 54, FAINT)
    out = (g + txt(53, IC - 40, "128²×3", 9, FAINT)
           + txt(53, IC + 48, "camera frame", 10.5, MUT, 600)
           + txt(53, IC + 60, "one night exposure", 9.5, FAINT)
           + txt(53, IC + 73, "frozen contract", 8.5, FAINT,
                 style="font-style='italic'"))
    return out, 26 + w


def output_part(x, sub="position fix + confidence"):
    return (txt(x, IC - 18, "frozen contract", 8.5, FAINT, anchor="start",
                style="font-style='italic'")
            + txt(x, IC - 2, "(u, v, conf)", 13, MUT, 600, "start")
            + txt(x, IC + 12, sub, 9, FAINT, anchor="start"))


def conf_branch(bar_x, out_x):
    gx = bar_x + 4
    ex = out_x + 28
    out = [leader(gx, IC + 64, gx, 216, FAINT),
           gauge(gx, 230, MUT),
           txt(gx, 248, "confidence 0–1", 9, MUT),
           f"<path d='M {gx + 18},230 H {ex} V {IC + 26}' fill='none' "
           f"stroke='{FAINT}' stroke-width='0.8'/>",
           f"<path d='M {ex},{IC + 20} l -3,6 h 6 Z' fill='{FAINT}'/>",
           txt((gx + ex) / 2, 224, "may abstain instead of guessing", 8.5, FAINT,
               style="font-style='italic'")]
    return "".join(out)


def loss_note(x_from, y_from, x_lab, text, color, glyph=None):
    out = [leader(x_from, y_from, x_lab - 8, LOSS_Y - 16, color)]
    if glyph:
        out.append(glyph)
    out.append(txt(x_lab, LOSS_Y - 12, text[0], 10, color, 600, "start"))
    if len(text) > 1:
        out.append(txt(x_lab, LOSS_Y, text[1], 9.5, color, 400, "start"))
    return "".join(out)


def mnv3_group(x0):
    """The pretrained encoder: four representative output tensors of the
    9-stage MobileNetV3-Small stack, drawn in red (this experiment)."""
    sizes = [(46, 7), (34, 11), (24, 15), (17, 21)]
    out, x, faces = [], x0, []
    for (s, d) in sizes:
        g, w = slab(x, s, d, ACC)
        out.append(g)
        faces.append((x, s))
        x += w + 15
    x -= 15
    out.append(kernel_proj(26, 54, faces[0][0], faces[0][1], FAINT))
    for (x1, s1), (x2, s2) in zip(faces, faces[1:]):
        out.append(kernel_proj(x1, s1, x2, s2, ACC))
    xc = (x0 + x) / 2
    out.append(cap(xc, IC + 58, "MobileNetV3-Small encoder",
                   "9 stages: inverted-residual convs + squeeze-excite", name_color=ACC))
    out.append(txt(xc, IC + 82, "ImageNet-pretrained init, finetuned", 9.5, ACC))
    out.append(txt(x0 + 23, IC - 36, "64²×16", 9, FAINT))
    out.append(txt(x - 20, IC - 32, "8²×48", 9, FAINT))
    return "".join(out), x


b = [lanes()]
g, x = frame_part()
b.append(g)
b.append(harrow(x + 6, x + 30, IC))
x += 32
g, x = mnv3_group(x)
b.append(g)
b.append(harrow(x + 8, x + 30, IC))
x += 34

# GAP descriptor + layout code (exp 10 head, unchanged -> ink)
g, _ = bar(x, 40, ticks=8)
b.append(g)
bar_x = x
g, _ = bar(x + 22, 92, ticks=15)
b.append(g)
b.append(cap(x + 15, IC + 66, "48-d GAP ⊕ 512-d layout code",
             "texture average + what-sits-where (1×1 conv)"))
fx = x + 30
g, _ = bar(fx + 56, 92, ticks=15)
b.append(fan(fx, 92, fx + 56, 92))
b.append(g)
b.append(cap(fx + 60, IC - 62, "FC → 1024 logits", "softmax"))
x = fx + 70
b.append(harrow(x, x + 26, IC))
gx = x + 28
g, gs = gridsq(gx, 76, 8, INK, BUMP)
b.append(g)
b.append(cap(gx + 38, IC + 56, "probability field", "32×32 cells over the map"))
tx = gx + gs + 96
b.append(converge(gx, 76, tx, INK))
b.append(cap((gx + gs + tx) / 2 + 4, IC - 54, "soft-argmax over ALL cells",
             "answer = Σ probability · cell-center"))
b.append(output_part(tx + 18))
b.append(conf_branch(bar_x, tx + 18))

# training lane
b.append(loss_note(215, IC + 88, 60,
                   ("encoder weights born on ImageNet — 1.28 M photographs (BSD-3 checkpoint, vendored)",
                    "backbone finetunes at lr 1e-4, new head at 1e-3 — training adapts vision instead of inventing it"),
                   ACC))
tiny, _ = gridsq(586, 22, 6, OCH, ((3, 2, .6),), yc=LOSS_Y - 12, lw=1)
b.append(loss_note(gx + 38, IC + 70, 616,
                   ("Gaussian-CE + decode L2 + conf BCE (unchanged)",), OCH, glyph=tiny))
b.append(txt(968, 26, "red = this experiment: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small encoder",
             9.5, ACC, anchor="end", style="font-style='italic'"))

svg = (f"<svg viewBox='0 0 980 340' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'>"
       + "".join(b) + "</svg>")

stages = [
    dict(name="Camera frame",
         detail="128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions",
         changed=False),
    dict(name="Feature extractor",
         detail="REPLACED: instead of a small CNN learning to see from zero on this map alone, an off-the-shelf MobileNetV3-Small — pre-trained on 1.28 million everyday photographs — turns the image into an 8×8 grid of 48 learned features; it arrives already knowing edges, textures and shapes, and only fine-tunes to this area",
         changed=True),
    dict(name="Layout summary",
         detail="a slim extra layer condenses that grid into a 512-number code that remembers what sits where in the crop; the 48-number average (which forgets position) is kept alongside",
         changed=False),
    dict(name="Probability map",
         detail="scores every cell of a 32×32 grid over the map: “how likely did the photo come from here?” — reading the layout code together with the texture average",
         changed=False),
    dict(name="Decode",
         detail="answer = balance point of the whole heat map (weighted average of all 1,024 cells)",
         changed=False),
    dict(name="Confidence",
         detail="separate 0–1 score; low means the model abstains rather than guessing",
         changed=False),
    dict(name="Output", detail="(lat, lon, confidence)", changed=False),
    dict(name="Training signal",
         detail="same losses as before (heat-map match + distance + confidence); the pre-trained eye is tuned 10× more gently than the new head so its general vision is not erased",
         changed=True, train_only=True),
]

exp = dict(
    title="ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small encoder replaces the from-scratch conv stack",
    category="architecture",
    hypothesis=(
        "The binding constraint is low-level feature quality, not the head, supervision, data volume, "
        "or capacity: ten experiments varying decode (3), dense supervision (4), hierarchical loss (5), "
        "rotation (6), data scale (7), capacity (8), contrastive signal (9) and head structure (10) all "
        "landed within ~10% of each other near the 3.2 km center-guess floor, and the shared invariant is "
        "that every design trained its texture filters from scratch inside the frozen ~4,500-step budget — "
        "far too few steps to invent low-level vision, which is why probability fields stay near-uniform in "
        "every lighting bucket. Exps 8, 9 and 10's conclusions all explicitly nominated pretrained texture "
        "features as the next suspect. ImageNet-pretrained features start with generic edge/texture/shape "
        "selectivity known to transfer across domains (including overhead imagery), so keeping the exp-10 "
        "head, losses, and data identical while swapping only the encoder init+topology directly tests "
        "whether feature quality is what has been limiting every design. This is the §3/§9 'permissively-"
        "licensed pretrained init' design family, untouched in 10 experiments."),
    method=(
        "Replace the from-scratch 4-conv encoder in model/model.py with the first nine feature stages of "
        "MobileNetV3-Small (stem + 8 inverted-residual blocks with squeeze-excitation, stride 16, 8×8×48 "
        "output on the frozen 128 px input), re-implemented in pure PyTorch with torchvision-matching "
        "module names and initialized with strict=True from a vendored 832 KB BSD-3 ImageNet checkpoint "
        "(model/pretrained/mnv3s_features8.pt) — no new dependency, no network at train time. ImageNet "
        "normalization is baked into forward() so the [0,1] ONNX contract is unchanged. The exp-10 head is "
        "kept identical in structure (GAP ⊕ 512-d layout code → 1024-cell field → soft-argmax; GAP width "
        "48 instead of 128 follows the encoder's output). model/train.py finetunes the backbone at lr 1e-4 "
        "vs 1e-3 for the new head. Verified pre-registration: strict weight load, ONNX 2.94 MiB < 4 MiB "
        "gate, host latency proxy 0.53 ms < 250 ms, torch↔ONNX max diff 1.5e-7, MPS training step OK."),
    expected_outcome=(
        "If feature quality is the binding constraint, worst-case median error drops clearly below the kept "
        "2299.74 m — plausibly 1,200–2,000 m, with bright buckets improving most (their texture is richest "
        "and closest to ImageNet statistics), i.e. the first non-flat lighting profile. Downside bounded: "
        "head, decode and losses are unchanged and a near-uniform field still decodes near the map center, "
        "so a null lands around 2.3–2.5 km. Watch-item: night buckets are farthest from ImageNet's photo "
        "statistics and may lag — that per-bucket spread is itself informative. A null would be decisive: "
        "with pretrained features, supervision form, rotation, data scale, capacity and head structure all "
        "ruled out, the remaining suspects are the information content of the relit imagery itself "
        "(learned relighting) and lighting-specialist structure."),
    init_strategy="pretrained:mobilenet_v3_small IMAGENET1K_V1 features[0:9] (BSD-3), finetuned",
    eli5=(
        "Until now, every experiment taught a brand-new artificial eye to see from scratch using only this "
        "one map — and in the short training time each experiment gets, it never learned to see sharply; "
        "ten different designs all stayed roughly equally blurry. This experiment swaps in an eye that has "
        "already spent a long apprenticeship on 1.28 million ordinary photographs, so it arrives knowing "
        "what edges, roofs, roads and trees look like. Training then only has to teach it this particular "
        "map, not vision itself — like hiring a trained scout and handing them the local map, instead of "
        "teaching a newborn both to see and to navigate in a week. If the model's problem really was "
        "'can't see well enough', this should be the biggest step so far; if nothing improves, we've "
        "learned the imagery itself, not the eye, is the limit."),
    architecture=dict(stages=stages),
    architecture_svg=svg,
)

with open("runs/pending_experiment.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(exp, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
print("svg bytes:", len(svg))
print("wrote runs/pending_experiment.json")
PYEOF
tool_input.description: Generate architecture SVG and write pending_experiment.json
